Records.authors: Bjerrum-Bohr, N.E. J., Damgaard, Poul H., Festuccia, Guido, Planté, Ludovic, Vanhove, Pierre
Records.description: One-loop scalar triangle integral with one massive propagator (mass $m_1=\sqrt{p_1^2}$) and two massless propagators, finite in $d=4$. Defined in the main text (line 168) as the building block for the classical part of the one-loop two-graviton scattering amplitude $M_2$ in Eq. (e:one). Fully evaluated in closed form in Appendix A using a Feynman parametric representation: integration over $x_1$ yields a logarithmic integrand, and integration over $x_2$ gives a result expressed via dilogarithms $\operatorname{Li}_2$ and logarithms involving the variables $\rho_\pm = -1/2 \pm (1/2)\sqrt{1 - 4 p_1^2/q^2}$. The large-mass limit $|q^2|/|p_1^2| \ll 1$ gives $I = (i/(32 q^2))\sqrt{-q^2/p_1^2} + \mathcal{O}(q^2/p_1^2)$, reproducing the leading classical contribution to general relativity through Eq. (e:Triresult).
Records.location: Eq. between (1)-(2) at line 168 (main text) and Appendix A (Eqs. (A.1)-(A.5))

Records.authors: Borowka, S., Heinrich, G., Jones, S.P., Kerner, M., Schlenk, J., Zirke, T.
Records.description: One-loop three-point function with one massive propagator and one off-shell leg ($p_3^2=s$). Demonstrates the user-defined integrand setup of SecDec-3.0 by feeding the three primary-sector parametric integrands $G_{i,user}(z_1,z_2)$ of Eqs. (17a-c) directly via functionlist, bypassing the standard Feynman-parameter pipeline. Total runtime 7 s.
